sections.heading: About this Task
sections.body:
You can export your pipeline to the following languages:

- C#

- Go

- Java

- Node

- PHP

- Python

- Ruby

- Rust

sections.heading: Step 1: Click .
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the aggregation pipeline pane, click the
  **Export Code** button to open the pipeline
  export card.

![Aggregation Builder export button](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-export-dropdown.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Select your export language.
sections.body:
On the right side of the card, click the drop-down menu under
**Exported Pipeline** and select your desired programming
language.

The **My Pipeline** pane on the left of the export card displays
your pipeline in [mongosh](mongodb-shell) syntax. The
**Exported Pipeline** pane to the right displays your pipeline in
the selected programming language.

sections.heading: Step 3: (Optional) Include import statements.
sections.body:
Click the **Include Import Statements** checkbox to include the
required import statements for the selected programming language.

sections.heading: Step 4: (Optional) Include driver syntax.
sections.body:
Click the **Include Driver Syntax** checkbox to include
application code for the selected programming language. If you include
driver syntax, the copyable code reflects [project](atlas-ui-query-bar-project), [sort by a field](atlas-ui-query-bar-sort), [maxTimeMS](atlas-ui-query-bar-max-time-ms), [collation](atlas-ui-query-bar-collation),
[skip](atlas-ui-query-bar-skip) and [limit](atlas-ui-query-bar-limit) options.

sections.heading: Step 5: Click the file  icon.
sections.body:
Click the files-o icon at the top-right of the pipeline to
copy your pipeline for the selected programming language. You can now
integrate and execute your created pipeline in your application.
